programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J(n)

programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville hear from drivers stranded abroad despite having paid for what they thought was comprehensive insurance. Plus, is carbon offsetting just a load of 'greenwashing'?(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits an old workshop in Margate in Kent and a building plot in Newcastle-under-Lyme. Martel Maxwell is at a semi-detached house in Wednesbury, West Midlands, that has some worrying cracks(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Richelle and her mother Lorraine, who were deciding on a move to Auckland, New Zealand and visited a selection of properties in the city(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reports on the determined sisters who set up a camera to expose whoever was stealing from their grandmother. Plus, how burglars left clues amidst the chaos when they ransacked a jeweller's and the trio of fly-tippers who dumped themselves right in it(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is joined by two teams led by experts Charles Hanson and David Harper at an antiques fair in Newbury, Berkshire, hoping to make a profit from their purchases at auction. The presenter also has a chat with a collector of pop concert tickets(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi gets drawn into the murky side of the prison. Emma has to take a stand when there is a patient revolt(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After the body of Reginald Brody is discovered at the bottom of Helmsley Tower, Chief Inspector Valentine returns to Kembleford to investigate. Suspecting that there is more to his death than suicide, Valentine enlists Father Brown to help his inquiries. Mark Williams and Hugo Speer star in the crime drama(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson finds himself back in Somerset with a couple whose rural aspirations needed to accommodate their own needs, plus those of their parents. Visiting the day before a local beer and cider festival, he earns a brew by helping the neighbours set up for the event, but not before checking out the wonderful home he helped them to find. Later, Jules then travels to Leicestershire to meet a husband and wife at their impressive new home and discovers how their move away from a large urban town has helped strengthen family ties(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hardworking families across the UK who work around the clock to make their farms a success compete to win the much coveted and prestigious title of best 'Farming Family Business' at the British Farming Awards. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer set sail on the Solent for an Isle of Wight antiques hunt. Both uncover silver goodies, but Charlie Ross manages to rip his trousers. At the auction at Battle, East Sussex, Izzie's silver brooch goes against Charlie's silver pen(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain sprinter Adam Gemili, tennis star Heather Watson, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott and ex-West Ham United striker Dean Ashton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush is thrilled to learn Sonia and Martin are back together but when the latter arrives home he finds a letter that leaves him rocked. Honey forces herself to eat in front of the children, making herself feel unwell, while Dotty continues to try to charm Leo - only for him to show his true colours when she mentions his father. Jean and Daniel are the happiest they have ever been, and Rainie sets up a date for Callum(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society undertakes a 1960s-set horror tale, with Robert as the mysterious Albert Fortenoy welcoming a young family to his crumbling old house. A number of clever theatrical horror devices are used to create a creepy atmosphere and plenty of jump scares - and every single one manages to go horribly wrong. Worse, the designers have taken 'crumbling' to heart and the cast members proceed to fall off, out of and through everything. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders and Joe Sugg join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Gary's under-9s little league team, The Badgers, are in the cup final. However, the week before the big match his star player, Abboussi, is poached by Chelsea. Gary throws absolutely everything at getting him back and even turns up, unannounced, at Abboussi's father's workplace to try to convince him that his son is making a big mistake. Big Gary bets against his own son's side to earn a few quid, and Terri is stumped to learn mother-in-law, Denise, is new best buddies with Chloe Ferdinando, Comedy, starring Tom Davis and Laura Checkley, with Simon Day, Camille Coduri and Romesh Ranganathan(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Hollywood stars Jamie Foxx and Michael B Jordan discuss starring in legal drama Just Mercy, while actress and comedian Jennifer Saunders talks about new mystery thriller series The Stranger, in which she features. Plus, Patrick Stewart reflects on his decision to reprise his most famous role in latest sci-fi spin-off Star Trek: Picard. Michael Kiwanuka performs Hero and more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The queens' acting skills are put to the test as they star in a sumptuous new comedy costume drama - Downton Draggy. Game of Thrones star Maisie Williams joins singer Michelle Visage and chat show presenter Graham Norton on the judging panel as events lead to another contender exiting the competition(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Lee Price has dreamt of living in Australia ever since getting married there, so he, his wife Amanda and their children head Down Under for a trial week. Nicki Chapman presents(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two inner-city London charities head to the 46th Lambeth Country Show. This is their chance to raise awareness, funds and support so they can continue their vital community work(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam goes on ‘Staycation' in the North of England. Beginning his adventure at a living museum, he achieves his dream of driving a steam train. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley reveal how fake car insurance is leaving thousands of people driving uninsured on Britain's roads without their knowledge. Plus, the health supplements that are totally useless, containing no active ingredients, the children's toys that contain gender-altering chemicals and tips on recognising fake modern collectibles(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Kyren Wilson v Jack Lisowski. Coverage of the first-round match on the fourth day at Alexandra Palace in London. Wilson was a runner-up in 2018, while Lisowski made his debut in the competition last year, when he was defeated by Ding Junhui at this stage. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Pubs That Built Britain
programme.desc: Si and Dave take a Manchester pub crawl to uncover historic establishments once at the heart of the city's industrial revolution. Starting in Middleton, they discover that politics, protest and plotting were rife in a pub's back rooms in the early 1800s, before visiting a local brewery to find out more about the popularity of beer in Britain(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Elizabeth Talbot and Christina Trevanion assess items brought into Allerton Castle, North Yorkshire, including two Moorcroft vases and a ruby necklace(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches Bodmin en route to Totnes, as he explores the West Country from St Ives to Salisbury Plain. Out on the rugged moor, Michael hears how the celebrated author Daphne du Maurier captivated readers between the wars with her tales of smuggling at the Jamaica Inn. In Devon, Michael takes the plunge at Plymouth's beautiful art deco Tinside Lido, while at Ivybridge, he boards a vehicle like no other to cross the causeway to Burgh Island, where a 1930s playboy built a splendid art deco party palace(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest on Brexit with Andrew Neil and guests(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: As Storm Ophelia hits the British Isles, on the south east coast of Ireland the pagers of the Rosslare crew go off, to attend to a small yacht with three people on board that is caught in one of the worst storms in living memory. In Devon, the crew at Appledore race an incoming tide to try to reach a man stuck fast in quicksand, while on the north coast of Cornwall, the crew of St Agnes must battle through huge breaking waves to reach two young boys being swept out to sea in a deadly rip current(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley and a team find out if eating less food can boost your memory, and Dr Javid Abdelmoneim investigates over-the-counter cannabis-based health products. Elsewhere, Zoe Williams reveals how to spot a hidden illness that can cause stillbirth, and Alain Gregoire discovers the body's secret built-in stress reliever(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: It's the first full week of the challenge and Tom is back in Gloucester, the city where he grew up, to exercise and cook alongside his diet and fitness gang. As team chef, he wants to get them off to a good start on calorie counting - and familiar, comforting dishes is the way to go. Window cleaning brothers-in-law Dan and Alex volunteered for the challenge because of health concerns and a normal day sees them tucking into a big, calorific breakfast and grabbing fast food between jobs, but Tom joins the boys on a bike ride in the local countryside before giving them a tasty breakfast muffin to try(n)

programme.title: Good Omens
programme.desc: Angel Aziraphale and demon Crowley are enjoying their lives on Earth and have become firm friends, but their comfortable existence is disrupted when Crowley is summoned by two demons, Hastur and Ligur, and presented with a newborn baby who is the Antichrist. The two friends vow to collaborate to ensure the boy will not bring about the end of the world. Neil Gaiman's adaptation of his and Terry Pratchett's fantasy adventure, starring Michael Sheen and David Tennant(n)

programme.title: Terry Pratchett: Back in Black
programme.desc: A dramatised documentary telling the story of the life of Terry Pratchett, the fantasy author whose popular Discworld series made him a household name, selling more then 85 million copies worldwide. In 2015, following a prolonged and very public battle with Alzheimers, Terry died, leaving behind one last unfinished story - his own. Here, actor Paul Kaye steps into the celebrated writer's shoes, charting Terry's life from his troubled schooldays and his difficulties securing the respect of literary critics, through to his eventual success, and his gradual decline into ill health. The film makes use of Terry's own words, along with those of his friend and collaborator Neil Gaiman, contemporary Val McDermid, and long-serving assistant Rob Wilkins(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Mark Williams v Stuart Bingham. Action from the first-round match on the fourth day at Alexandra Palace in London. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Kyren Wilson v Jack Lisowski. Extended highlights of the first-round match on the fourth day at Alexandra Palace in London(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam goes on ‘Staycation' in the North of England. Beginning his adventure at a living museum, he achieves his dream of driving a steam train. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Murder Trial: The Disappearance of Margaret Fleming
programme.desc: Two-part documentary about the investigation and trial of two carers accused of murdering Margaret Fleming and claiming benefits in her name for 16 years, taking audiences to the centre of the trial at Glasgow's High Court and offering a compelling insight into the work of the police and prosecutors in bringing the two suspects to trial(n)

programme.title: Murder Trial: The Disappearance of Margaret Fleming
programme.desc: Part two of two. The trial of Edward Cairney and Avril Jones continues, including the testimonies of Jean McSherry, the former partner of Margaret's father Derick, and Margaret's former teacher Jacqueline Cahill. The programme also follows senior investigating officer Detective Superintendent Paul Livingstone as he revisits Margaret's derelict home in Seacroft, Inverkip(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with mum Persephone and partner Debbie, who sampled a week in New Zealand. One year on, cameras reveal whether they decided to make the move(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: First-time farmers Paige and Kingsley are hoping that showing their sheep at the Isle of Wight show will bring them closer to their life dream of becoming full-time farmers(n)

programme.title: Baby Chimp Rescue
programme.desc: Documentary following life in a home for orphaned chimpanzees run by wildlife vet Jimmy Desmond and his wife Jenny. The Desmonds started with just two rescued baby chimps, saved from the illegal pet trade, but now their home is bursting at the seams with 21 orphans to look after. Their dream is to build them all a new sanctuary in the forest and, with help from friend and chimp specialist Ben Garrod, they teach the orphans the skills they would have learnt from their mothers in the wild(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the opening quarter-final on the fifth day at Alexandra Palace in London. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Pubs That Built Britain
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers are in Cornwall, discovering pubs that were at the centre of the smuggling industry back in the late 1700s. They start out in the tiny fishing village of Polperro, discovering a smugglers' tunnel and a landlord with criminal connections. Heading inland to Bodmin Moor they visit the famous Jamaica Inn, where they find out how to make the famous tipple of the time - rum shrub(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From Capesthorne Hall in Cheshire, where Mark Stacey finds a horse ledger that is a runaway success and Caroline Hawley uncovers a set of letters from Sir Arthur Conan Doyle(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their general knowledge skills in trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Paignton, south Devon, where the Dartmouth Steam Railway takes him to the home of Agatha Christie, in the company of her great-grandson James Prichard(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the second quarter-final on the fifth day at Alexandra Palace in London. Recently crowned UK champion Ding Junhui edged past Luca Brecel at this stage last year, coming back from 5-4 down to prevail 6-5. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Baby Chimp Rescue
programme.desc: There are more new arrivals at the home, meaning Jimmy and Jenny Desmond now have 28 rescued chimpanzees to look after, including an eight-week-old baby called Gaia. Plans are coming together for the new sanctuary in the forest, and the orphans learn to master tools so they can forage for food. But a deadly cold breaks out among the babies, with disastrous consequences. The team also rescues an adult male chimp called Jonny who brings a fresh set of challenges. The pressure is on to move the chimps to their new home before things get out of hand(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: In America, it's an epidemic. Now new evidence from Public Health England raises concern about the UK's use of prescription opioids. Dr Michael Mosley investigates this growing problem, meeting patients struggling with addiction and GPs fighting a constant battle to help those suffering from chronic pain. He also uncovers worrying evidence of people abusing over-the-counter opioids and discovers how easy it is to buy strong opioids online. Michael also considers why people are consuming twice the amount of opioids as 20 years ago, and asks the crucial question - do they actually work?(n)

programme.title: Mum
programme.desc: Cathy and her family arrive in the countryside to celebrate Derek's birthday. Michael is there too, which Jason finds troubling. Family comedy, starring Lesley Manville, Peter Mullan, Sam Swainsbury and Ross Boatman(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents action from the second quarter-final on the fifth day at Alexandra Palace in London. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents extended highlights of the opening quarter-final on the fifth day at Alexandra Palace in London(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Documentary about the Cornish fishing village of Mevagissey. While many fishing communities are struggling, this one has thrived owing to protection from prevailing winds and returning fish stocks. Boats have been handed down through generations and currently 74 operate out of the harbour. As young captains prepare to follow in their father's footsteps, tourism also becomes a major boost to the town - but leaves it feeling deserted in winter. A veteran skipper prepares to retire, and questions what the next generation will learn from his example(n)

programme.title: Snow Cats and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Buchanan and Dr Lukarevsky keep a close eye on Koshka's family as they continue on the road to full recovery. They have to prove their ability to hunt and survive before they can be released back to the wild. Bryansk has become so domesticated it's a major challenge for Gordon and Victor to help him find his wild instincts. If he cannot he will not be able to be released and will have to spend the rest of his life in captivity(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood takes on his toughest challenge yet, working as a paramedic in Austin, Texas. His mentor is Cassi Lydon, a veteran paramedic with 14 years' service(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Holly Walker wants to start a new life in Australia, and hopes a trial week in sun-kissed Nelson, Victoria, will persuade husband Rob it is worth the move. Nicki Chapman presents(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: International sheepdog breeder David and farmer Brenda battle the temperamental British weather with their pedigree dogs by their sides, competing in Powburn, Northumberland, at one of Britain's oldest sheepdog trials(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the last heat of the competition, the first three chefs recreate Monica's dish of oeufs meurette, before the next three take on Marcus' drop scones and a suzette sauce. In the next round, the six chefs are given a chance to showcase their creativity and cooking style as they present a dish of their own design to the judges(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the third quarter-final on the sixth day at Alexandra Palace in London. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Pubs That Built Britain
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers take a tour of Lincolnshire's pubs, and discover the important roles many of the county's establishments played in the Second World War, from a pub that was used as temporary accommodation for people made homeless by bombing to the Bluebell Inn in Woodhall Spa, which was the local for the pilots of the Dambuster squadron(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Newby Hall, North Yorkshire. Mark Stacey comes across two unusual tie and stick pins and Claire Rawle enthuses about a large collection of toy cars(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the county line from Devon into Somerset, seeing first-hand how willow farmers sought to overcome the challenge from the production of synthetic plastics during the 1930s and hearing how tourism has spread across the region, especially amongst the young. In Bath, Michael visits the former home of a refugee emperor, whose country was invaded by the Italian dictator Mussolini, and in whose name a religious movement began that now flourishes worldwide. Travelling east into Wiltshire, he reaches the largest training area of the British Army, Salisbury Plain(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents coverage of the fourth quarter-final on the sixth day at Alexandra Palace in London, played over the best of 11 frames. Neil Robertson was a 6-3 winner over Barry Hawkins at this stage last year, despite losing the first two frames of the match. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The horticulturalist explores gardens of early independent America, learning how the history of the South is still very much evident in the places and people he meets. He visits the famous garden where President Thomas Jefferson, one of America's founding fathers, enjoyed spending his time seeking out seeds and plants for his 1,000-ft vegetable garden. From there he heads to Charleston, which boasts some of the most beautiful trees in the world and learns about the life of the plantations there. Later, in Miami, Monty visits one of the most spectacular Renaissance recreations built on marshland(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig finds herself stuck in a 'Quagmire', as she invites Sindhu Vee, Sally Phillips and Aisling Bea to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish posers. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Eshaan Akbar, Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Rhys James and Sindhu Vee and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another. First shown in November(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard and her panel discuss the links between culture, money and power. In the wake of recent protests against multinational corporations funding some of the nation's major cultural institutions, they question whether landmark exhibitions, literary prizes and theatre productions should be backed by big business, as opposed to arguably more 'ethical' ways of funding the arts. The programme includes an exclusive interview with Bank of England Governor Mark Carney as he prepares to stand down from his post(n)

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents action from the fourth quarter-final on the sixth day at Alexandra Palace in London. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: James May: The Reassembler
programme.desc: James is faced with reassembling a 1970s Honda Z50A Mini Trail Motorcycle from all its 303 component parts. This exciting, portable motorcycle was fun for the whole family and had a generation of young people hooked on motorcycles for the rest of their lives(n)

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: Following the astonishing wildlife, landscapes and people of the world's longest river, the Nile, beginning in the tropical mountain range of the Rwenzoris, home to one of the world's strangest birds, the shoebill stork. Then, cameras follow the Nile's journey as it travels through the wild Savannah lands of Uganda, before reaching the Ethiopian highlands where it receives an injection of water far to the East(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The documentary charts the arrest, trial and conviction of Peter Sutcliffe, and the legacy for the relatives of his victims and the survivors of his attacks. The film explores how the trial was jeopardised when witnesses were offered money for exclusives and how the front seats in the court were reserved for VIPs. Going back to survivors of the attacks and relatives of Sutcliffe's victims, the film also explores the legacy left behind by his crimes and finds out what it has been like to live as the child of a Ripper victim(n)

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston explores the forensic advances that have elevated murder weapons from mere instruments to crucial pieces of evidence. She sees how arsenic, the undetectable weapon of choice in the 19th century, was exposed as the killer thanks to one simple chemical test, and finds out how distinctive marks on a victim's skull led to the weapon and, in turn, the killer. Gabriel also looks at the latest forensic developments, from 3-D laser scanning that can reconstruct the sequence of events at the scene of a gun crime to the pioneering chemistry able to determine where in the world someone has been based on just a few strands of their hair(n)

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke looks at art from one of Ancient Egypt's most opulent eras, beginning with the troubling psychological portraits of tyrant Senwosret III, who ruled during the Middle Kingdom, through to the golden mask of Tutankhamun, which was created hundreds of years later. He also explores architectural wonders, exquisite tombs and a lost city, where an artistic revolution took place and a new creative style was developed under Akhenaten and Nefertiti(n)

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke tells the stories of sculptors John Flaxman, Francis Chantrey and Alfred Gilbert, who bucked trends in the 18th and 19th centuries. He examines the technical breakthroughs behind their key works - such as carving in marble with a pointer machine and the lost-wax technique - which were produced at a time of increased interest in sculptural projects to celebrate the size of the British Empire(n)

programme.title: John Berger: The Art of Looking
programme.desc: Portrait of the writer and artist to mark his 90th birthday, examining his influential outlook on life and art and his creative collaborations in a variety of mediums, with contributions from theatrical practitioner Simon McBurney, director Michael Dibb, visual artist John Christie, cartoonist Selcuk Demiral, photographer Jean Mohur and two of Bergwer's children - critic Katya and painter Yves. The film is bookended by Berger's experience of cataract removal surgery and rediscovery of the power of seeing(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: A look at Hawaiian influences on US roots music, including the invention of the steel guitar by Joseph Kekuku, and the development of Cajun music in Louisiana. Plus, the story of one of the genre's unsung heroes, Mississippi John Hurt, who became a star in the 1920s, only to return to life as a share cropper in the Great Depression, before a revival of his music in the 1960s(n)

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: Following the astonishing wildlife, landscapes and people of the world's longest river, the Nile, beginning in the tropical mountain range of the Rwenzoris, home to one of the world's strangest birds, the shoebill stork. Then, cameras follow the Nile's journey as it travels through the wild Savannah lands of Uganda, before reaching the Ethiopian highlands where it receives an injection of water far to the East(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team goes back to basics to show how to enjoy the night sky, wherever you are, and Pete Lawrence joins a group of novice stargazers to talk about the best ways to introduce newcomers to the night. Chris Lintott ventures onto a roof in Oxford and marvels at the moon through binoculars, and Maggie Aderin-Pocock goes back to her childhood telescope-making class to talk basic telescope essentials(n)

programme.title: The Great British Year
programme.desc: Documentary following British wildlife through the seasons. Spring marks a series of beginnings, as trees explode with blossom and mornings fill with the chorus of birdsong. Long-tailed tits frantically build nests, a stoat mother hunts rabbits to feed her playful young, while in the oceans, seahorses sway to a graceful courtship dance. As spring becomes summer, guillemot chicks leap from their cliffs to begin life at sea. Narrated by Joseph Fiennes(n)

programme.title: Einstein's Quantum Riddle
programme.desc: The story of perhaps the strangest idea in science, quantum entanglement, the concept that a pair of group of particles that had interacted in the past will continue to act in unison - even if they are separated by millions of miles. This documentary reveals how the idea began when Einstein helped to usher in quantum mechanics in the early 20th century. But no one seemed able to prove the scientist's theories - until 30 years later(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox explores how the development of mankind and the history of the universe are intertwined, visiting dramatic locations around the world to illustrate the fundamental scientific principles that govern the laws of nature. He begins by investigating time, charting the evolution of the cosmos and explaining why the universe must one day come to an end(n)

programme.title: Michael Mosley vs the Superbugs
programme.desc: With more bacteria fighting back against antibiotics, Michael Mosley goes in search of the cause of this crisis and carries out an unprecedented experiment, using a life-size bacterial clone of himself, to find out whether new treatments can overcome(n)

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke tells the story of sculpture over the past 100 years, a century of innovation, scandal and creativity. He examines the artistic revolution spearheaded by Eric Gill and Jacob Epstein, which paved the way for the work of Gilbert & George and Damien Hirst. He also explores the art form's rise in popularity, from First World War memorials to the landmark work of Anthony Gormley and Rachel Whiteread(n)

programme.title: Bought with Love: The Secret History of British Art Collections
programme.desc: Art historian Helen Rosslyn continues to explore impressive country houses, focusing on the Grand Tour era of the 18th century when aristocrats filled their homes with masterpieces by 17th-century classical painters. She visits Holkham Hall in Norfolk to view the collection there, explores the legacy of the Dukes of Richmond at Goodwood House, and learns how the one-time Lord Egremont of Petworth House in Sussex patronised JMW Turner(n)

programme.title: A History of Art in Three Colours
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox follows up his British Masters series by investigating artists' use of blue, white and gold, and explains how, by choosing these colours, painters have stirred emotions, changed the way people behave and had an impact on the course of history. He begins with gold and its use in early sun worship, before reinterpreting the treasures of the pharaohs, the mosaics of Byzantium and the work of Renaissance master Benvenuto Cellini. He also explores Gustav Klimt's The Kiss and discovers a Birmingham inventor who tried to achieve the alchemist's dream - turning base metals into gold(n)

programme.title: Einstein's Quantum Riddle
programme.desc: The story of perhaps the strangest idea in science, quantum entanglement, the concept that a pair of group of particles that had interacted in the past will continue to act in unison - even if they are separated by millions of miles. This documentary reveals how the idea began when Einstein helped to usher in quantum mechanics in the early 20th century. But no one seemed able to prove the scientist's theories - until 30 years later(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Richard Skinner present the edition first shown on January 19, featuring performances by Roachford, Roy Orbison, Robert Howard and Kym Mazelle, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, Ten City, Mica Paris and Will Downing, New Model Army, Brother Beyond, Natalie Cole, Mike and the Mechanics, Holly Johnson, Fine Young Cannibals, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan, and Rob Base and DJ E-Z Rock(n)

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The composer and author reveals how the modern shape of the musical was established through a series of pioneering works, examining Jerome Kern and Oscar Hammerstein's Show Boat's bold look at America's racial divide in the 1920s. He also explores innovative uses of songs that further the narrative, referencing Alan Jay Lerner and Frederick Loewe's My Fair Lady, which made a star of Julie Andrews in the late 1950s(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Anthea Turner present the edition first shown on January 26, featuring performances by Then Jerico, Adeva, Rob Base and DJ E-Z Rock, Mica Paris and Will Downing, Bobby Brown, Level 42, Sheena Easton, Ten City, Brother Beyond, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, and Milli Vanilli(n)

programme.title: Stewart Copeland's Adventures in Music
programme.desc: The musician explores the power music has to bring people together and to bond them in ways that are fundamental. His travels take him from the southern German cave where a 40,000-year-old bone flute was discovered to the modern-day mass singalong of New York's Choir! Choir! Choir! Along the way he gets to play with a Memphis marching band, join a song circle led by Bobby McFerrin, deconstruct the sexiness of Relax with its producer Trevor Horn, discuss the art of song-writing with his old Police colleague Sting and learn how to create dance floor unity with star DJ Honey Dijon(n)

programme.title: Duran Duran: There's Something You Should Know
programme.desc: Simon Le Bon, John Taylor, Nick Rhodes and Roger Taylor open up about the highs and lows of the band's career - with archive footage of former guitarist Andy Taylor helping round out the story. The documentary charts their trajectory through seven of their 14 albums, each one uncovering a compelling chapter in the history of five lads from Birmingham who had one simple dream - to become the biggest band in the world. Contributors include Boy George, Nile Rodgers and Mark Ronson(n)

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Musicians Midge Ure and Kim Appleby visit London and Manchester, the two cities that did battle with each other for musical pre-eminence as '80s music turned towards the new sounds of dance. This is a tale of how studio technology changed music - with British bands putting their own unique spin on dance to produce contrasting northern and southern sounds. Contributors include Denise Pearson from Five Star, Soul II Soul's Jazzie B, Mark Moore of S-Express, Shaun Ryder from the Happy Mondays and Peter Hook of New Order(n)

programme.desc: Maddie Moate visits an indoor water park and uses a special waterproof camera and animation to show how a water slide works(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jet Cadets take to the skies in Argentina in an attempt to pass their Academy Gliding Test. As gliders do not have engines, Xuli is teaching two other pilot cadets how to glide using thermals to keep them up in the air(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Brooklyn-Bells take Waffle to Mrs Hobbs' house, hoping to show her he's well behaved. With the voice of Rufus Hound(n)

programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: International sheepdog breeder David and farmer Brenda battle the temperamental British weather with their pedigree dogs by their sides, competing in Powburn, Northumberland, at one of Britain's oldest sheepdog trials(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: After a quick lesson in how to identify different wood, Izzie Balmer gambles on some furniture, while Charlie Ross is excited to find a rare antique he has never seen before. Who will make the most profit at auction in Sidmouth?(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is pleased when Dennis continues to ignore Sharon but when he spots the police outside Karen's he begins to worry. Martin and Sonia enjoy keeping their relationship a secret but Sonia's good mood is short-lived when hit-and-run victim George keeps pressuring her to pay for his silence, prompting her to do the unthinkable. Honey gets a wake-up call when her actions affect Janet, prompting her to open up to Billy, and Linda struggles at work when Daniel offers her a drink(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Week three of the culinary sees Mary Berry ask the contestants to produce their ultimate Sunday brunch, after which Chris Bavin challenges them to rustle up a dish in just one hour using a sweet ingredient. They all push themselves to the limits to impress the judges, knowing that whoever serves up the least popular dishes have to compete in the eliminator round, for which chef Angela Hartnett has selected a technically complicated recipe(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Jack Mooney and the team are called to a workshop where acclaimed artist Donna Harman has been found dead. Suspicion immediately falls on the three people closest to the crime scene, but with no one entering the workshop between the time the victim returned to start on her next painting and being found dead, the scene paints a tricky picture. Only a solitary drink can seems to hold the answer, but matters are complicated when Donna's art dealer and an art collector enter the fray. Meanwhile, Jack is confused by his blossoming feelings for Anna. Whodunit, starring Ardal O'Hanlon, with Nina Wadia, Barbara Flynn, Louise Brealey and Vivienne Acheampong(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Liverpool, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville hear from drivers stranded abroad despite having paid for what they thought was comprehensive insurance. Plus, is carbon offsetting just a load of 'greenwashing'?(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits an old workshop in Margate in Kent and a building plot in Newcastle-under-Lyme. Martel Maxwell is at a semi-detached house in Wednesbury, West Midlands, that has some worrying cracks(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Richelle and her mother Lorraine, who were deciding on a move to Auckland, New Zealand and visited a selection of properties in the city(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reports on the determined sisters who set up a camera to expose whoever was stealing from their grandmother. Plus, how burglars left clues amidst the chaos when they ransacked a jeweller's and the trio of fly-tippers who dumped themselves right in it(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is joined by two teams led by experts Charles Hanson and David Harper at an antiques fair in Newbury, Berkshire, hoping to make a profit from their purchases at auction. The presenter also has a chat with a collector of pop concert tickets(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi gets drawn into the murky side of the prison. Emma has to take a stand when there is a patient revolt(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After the body of Reginald Brody is discovered at the bottom of Helmsley Tower, Chief Inspector Valentine returns to Kembleford to investigate. Suspecting that there is more to his death than suicide, Valentine enlists Father Brown to help his inquiries. Mark Williams and Hugo Speer star in the crime drama(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson finds himself back in Somerset with a couple whose rural aspirations needed to accommodate their own needs, plus those of their parents. Visiting the day before a local beer and cider festival, he earns a brew by helping the neighbours set up for the event, but not before checking out the wonderful home he helped them to find. Later, Jules then travels to Leicestershire to meet a husband and wife at their impressive new home and discovers how their move away from a large urban town has helped strengthen family ties(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hardworking families across the UK who work around the clock to make their farms a success compete to win the much coveted and prestigious title of best 'Farming Family Business' at the British Farming Awards. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer set sail on the Solent for an Isle of Wight antiques hunt. Both uncover silver goodies, but Charlie Ross manages to rip his trousers. At the auction at Battle, East Sussex, Izzie's silver brooch goes against Charlie's silver pen(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dedicated vegan Mathew Pritchard takes to the dance floor to make a vegan meal that will get a troupe of ballroom dancers foxtrotting to a straight 10. With a sprinkle of flare, a dollop of panache and a pinch of style, Matt aims to show the waltzing wonders how plant-based food can stand up to any conventional British classic dish(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush is thrilled to learn Sonia and Martin are back together but when the latter arrives home he finds a letter that leaves him rocked. Honey forces herself to eat in front of the children, making herself feel unwell, while Dotty continues to try to charm Leo - only for him to show his true colours when she mentions his father. Jean and Daniel are the happiest they have ever been, and Rainie sets up a date for Callum(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society undertakes a 1960s-set horror tale, with Robert as the mysterious Albert Fortenoy welcoming a young family to his crumbling old house. A number of clever theatrical horror devices are used to create a creepy atmosphere and plenty of jump scares - and every single one manages to go horribly wrong. Worse, the designers have taken 'crumbling' to heart and the cast members proceed to fall off, out of and through everything. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders and Joe Sugg join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: The Tuckers
programme.desc: Billy strikes a deal with Clock to buy some dodgy wine. Meanwhile, Glyn gets in trouble with a local gangster and his violent sons. Comedy, written by and starring Steve Speirs, with Robert Pugh, Alexandria Riley, Joshua McCord, Ben McGregor and Jams Thomas(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Hollywood stars Jamie Foxx and Michael B Jordan discuss starring in legal drama Just Mercy, while actress and comedian Jennifer Saunders talks about new mystery thriller series The Stranger, in which she features. Plus, Patrick Stewart reflects on his decision to reprise his most famous role in latest sci-fi spin-off Star Trek: Picard. Michael Kiwanuka performs Hero and more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Gary's under-9s little league team, The Badgers, are in the cup final. However, the week before the big match his star player, Abboussi, is poached by Chelsea. Gary throws absolutely everything at getting him back and even turns up, unannounced, at Abboussi's father's workplace to try to convince him that his son is making a big mistake. Big Gary bets against his own son's side to earn a few quid, and Terri is stumped to learn mother-in-law, Denise, is new best buddies with Chloe Ferdinando, Comedy, starring Tom Davis and Laura Checkley, with Simon Day, Camille Coduri and Romesh Ranganathan(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The queens' acting skills are put to the test as they star in a sumptuous new comedy costume drama - Downton Draggy. Game of Thrones star Maisie Williams joins singer Michelle Visage and chat show presenter Graham Norton on the judging panel as events lead to another contender exiting the competition(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain sprinter Adam Gemili, tennis star Heather Watson, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott and ex-West Ham United striker Dean Ashton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush is thrilled to learn Sonia and Martin are back together but when the latter arrives home he finds a letter that leaves him rocked. Honey forces herself to eat in front of the children, making herself feel unwell, while Dotty continues to try to charm Leo - only for him to show his true colours when she mentions his father. Jean and Daniel are the happiest they have ever been, and Rainie sets up a date for Callum(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society undertakes a 1960s-set horror tale, with Robert as the mysterious Albert Fortenoy welcoming a young family to his crumbling old house. A number of clever theatrical horror devices are used to create a creepy atmosphere and plenty of jump scares - and every single one manages to go horribly wrong. Worse, the designers have taken 'crumbling' to heart and the cast members proceed to fall off, out of and through everything. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders and Joe Sugg join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Gary's under-9s little league team, The Badgers, are in the cup final. However, the week before the big match his star player, Abboussi, is poached by Chelsea. Gary throws absolutely everything at getting him back and even turns up, unannounced, at Abboussi's father's workplace to try to convince him that his son is making a big mistake. Big Gary bets against his own son's side to earn a few quid, and Terri is stumped to learn mother-in-law, Denise, is new best buddies with Chloe Ferdinando, Comedy, starring Tom Davis and Laura Checkley, with Simon Day, Camille Coduri and Romesh Ranganathan(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Hollywood stars Jamie Foxx and Michael B Jordan discuss starring in legal drama Just Mercy, while actress and comedian Jennifer Saunders talks about new mystery thriller series The Stranger, in which she features. Plus, Patrick Stewart reflects on his decision to reprise his most famous role in latest sci-fi spin-off Star Trek: Picard. Michael Kiwanuka performs Hero and more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The queens' acting skills are put to the test as they star in a sumptuous new comedy costume drama - Downton Draggy. programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with mum Persephone and partner Debbie, who sampled a week in New Zealand. One year on, cameras reveal whether they decided to make the move(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac and Kian return to work and support each other as they try to conquer their demons, and Sacha's feelings for Essie are only being made worse by living together. Meanwhile, Serena tries to find a new role for herself, but with Jason coping well and a new face to contend with, she may find herself forced out(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. Investigating the murder of missing teenager Jason Forbes, Nikki and the Lyell team are forced to question police methods, as DCI Claire Ashby seems more concerned with building the case against her main suspect. With pressure building on the police to secure a conviction, the team must fight to ensure that inconvenient truths are not overlooked(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Paralympian Kadeena Cox and her ex Tes, and Geordie Shore star Sam Gowland and former partner TOWIE's Georgia Harrison, reunite to answer some awkward questions about their relationships. Georgia wants to know if their romance was for real and Kadeena Cox has some burning questions for Tes - but could they get back together?(n)

programme.title: My Mate's A Bad Date
programme.desc: Khalil from Nottingham cannot get a second date, and Oloni thinks that remembering his dates' names may help(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a semi-detached house in Romford, Essex, a four-bedroom terrace in Liverpool and a former bank in Nuneaton in Warwickshire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Holly Walker wants to start a new life in Australia, and hopes a trial week in sun-kissed Nelson, Victoria, will persuade husband Rob it is worth the move. Nicki Chapman presents(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A mother fears for her life as two men armed with an axe make their way towards her room, and a burglar makes a sartorial slip-up when his clothes are recognised by the police(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel manages to tie himself in a knot, while Jimmi finds himself posed with medical intrigue. Sid helps a patient whose wife has gone missing(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A gossip columnist is murdered after threatening to expose a fashion house's secrets, leaving the sleuthing priest with a fresh mystery to solve. Whodunit, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley revisits two couples who relocated to the countryside, chatting to a husband and wife who bought a home in the Peak District in 2018 and a fun-loving pair whose original plan to move to Shropshire changed. However, their new home is still a far cry from their former lives in the city(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: International sheepdog breeder David and farmer Brenda battle the temperamental British weather with their pedigree dogs by their sides, competing in Powburn, Northumberland, at one of Britain's oldest sheepdog trials(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: After a quick lesson in how to identify different wood, Izzie Balmer gambles on some furniture, while Charlie Ross is excited to find a rare antique he has never seen before. Who will make the most profit at auction in Sidmouth?(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is pleased when Dennis continues to ignore Sharon but when he spots the police outside Karen's he begins to worry. Martin and Sonia enjoy keeping their relationship a secret but Sonia's good mood is short-lived when hit-and-run victim George keeps pressuring her to pay for his silence, prompting her to do the unthinkable. Honey gets a wake-up call when her actions affect Janet, prompting her to open up to Billy, and Linda struggles at work when Daniel offers her a drink(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Week three of the culinary sees Mary Berry ask the contestants to produce their ultimate Sunday brunch, after which Chris Bavin challenges them to rustle up a dish in just one hour using a sweet ingredient. They all push themselves to the limits to impress the judges, knowing that whoever serves up the least popular dishes have to compete in the eliminator round, for which chef Angela Hartnett has selected a technically complicated recipe(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Jack Mooney and the team are called to a workshop where acclaimed artist Donna Harman has been found dead. Suspicion immediately falls on the three people closest to the crime scene, but with no one entering the workshop between the time the victim returned to start on her next painting and being found dead, the scene paints a tricky picture. Only a solitary drink can seems to hold the answer, but matters are complicated when Donna's art dealer and an art collector enter the fray. Meanwhile, Jack is confused by his blossoming feelings for Anna. Whodunit, starring Ardal O'Hanlon, with Nina Wadia, Barbara Flynn, Louise Brealey and Vivienne Acheampong(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For more than a century, the nation's high streets have been key to local communities, but with one in 10 shops sitting empty, they are in crisis. In this programme, business journalist Adam Shaw investigates the government's plans to spend millions of pounds reviving run-down town centres, and finds high streets across the country looking at other ways to remain part of their community. He learns that to adapt and change on the scale that is needed, they need to have more than just money(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: For the next leg of their journey, the actor and his son head to the Great Smoky Mountains on the Tennessee-North Carolina border, where they go canoeing on the iconic rapids that featured in the film Deliverance. With Bradley left in need of a whisky, they head to a Hillbilly Jam for a nightcap and to experience the mountain tradition of clog dancing, before the pair try their hand at chopping wood with some competitive lumberjacks and taking to the woods to search for a mythical mountain beast(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen listens in disbelief as Peter talks about alcoholism and invites her to join him at one of his meetings. programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: Stan and his number two Mick Clark speak to Jeremy and relatives of the murdered family, but their picture of what really happened at White House Farm remains inconclusive. The twins' father Colin Caffell is devastated by his loss, and is initially comforted by Jeremy, while post-mortem examinations reveal nothing to contradict the murder-suicide theory. Meanwhile, relative Ann Eaton cannot believe Sheila was responsible, and discusses her growing suspicions with her brother David Boutflour(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: A report on the downward trend in spending in Britain's high-street stores over the past 10 years. Geraint Vincent follows typical shopping street Dalton Road in Barrow-in-Furness through the crucial festive period and hears from firms facing high rates and tough competition from the internet and retail parks. But to some, high streets are more than just a place of business - they are a crucial part of the community. Geraint also meets those trying to attract more visitors, and explores whether everyone has a responsibility to support town centres(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jai begins to unravel, Kim and Jamie unite and Rhona confronts Marlon(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: For the final leg of his journey, Gino D'Acampo takes a railway adventure to picturesque Lake Iseo, a hidden gem often overshadowed by its big sisters Garda and Como. He starts out angling for sardines, then takes his catch and prepares a dish of grilled fish for the passengers of Treno dei Sapore - a tourist train that takes guests along the lakeside and into the Franciacorta region. While they enjoy the views they also get a seven-course meal - with today's starter cooked by Gino(n)

programme.title: Welcome to HMP Belmarsh with Ross Kemp
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ross Kemp's report from inside the maximum-security jail takes him to Belmarsh's jail-within-a-jail, where anti-Islamic inmates have to be separated from Islamic prisoners for fear of violence. He also heads to the prison healthcare unit, and meets transgender prisoner Claire Darbyshire, who was given one of the lowest minimum terms for murder in British legal history after killing her father(n)

programme.title: The Late Debate

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a student from a prestigious all-female college is found dead, Will and Geordie infiltrate the murky world of campus politics and university societies to find the killer. Navigating strange initiations, raucous parties - and with investigative journalist, Ellie Harding, hot on their heels - they need to use every advantage at their disposal to unravel the mystery. Cathy's ambitions for a life of her own start to test the delicate family balance, while fresh from a spell with Daniel in Marrakech, Leonard finds it hard to acclimatise to life back in Grantchester. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia visits the lush Ionian island of Corfu, beginning in its capital, Corfu Town, where she discovers a surprising, cosmopolitan city more like a little slice of Italy than Greece. After sampling the delights of its famous mumquat flavoured ice-cream, a cookery lesson in making signature savoury dish pastitsada reveals the island's centuries-old love affair with exotic spices. Julia meets a pair of brothers passionate about revolutionising production of Corfu's oldest and most important export - olive oil- and ends her stay trying out the secret to staying young that all Corfiots swear by(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Realising he's been set up by Bernie, Kel makes a grab for her phone. Jade confronts Fiz in the street, and tells her she never intended for her to lose her kids, but she's only got herself to blame. Over their game of chess, Roy puts pressure on Nina to move in(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: After Will and Leonard witness a deadly and deliberate hit-and-run, Geordie traces the car to a pair of decidedly dysfunctional brothers, whose house lies far off the beaten path. The victim's wife, his employee and the two brothers are all damaged characters caught in a web of lies and betrayals - and it's not just secrets hiding within the siblings' grounds. Back at home, Will and Geordie both struggle as unwelcome guests look set to stay - Will's mum Amelia seems to have found a carbon copy of his late father in her new beau. Meanwhile, a new television set lands in the vicarage - just in time for Eurovision. Robson Green, Tom Brittney and Al Weaver star(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: For the next leg of their journey, the actor and his son head to the Great Smoky Mountains on the Tennessee-North Carolina border, where they go canoeing on the iconic rapids that featured in the film Deliverance. With Bradley left in need of a whisky, they head to a Hillbilly Jam for a nightcap and to experience the mountain tradition of clog dancing, before the pair try their hand at chopping wood with some competitive lumberjacks and taking to the woods to search for a mythical mountain beast(n)

programme.desc: Yasmeen listens in disbelief as Peter talks about alcoholism and invites her to join him at one of his meetings. programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: Stan and his number two Mick Clark speak to Jeremy and relatives of the murdered family, but their picture of what really happened at White House Farm remains inconclusive. The twins' father Colin Caffell is devastated by his loss, and is initially comforted by Jeremy, while post-mortem examinations reveal nothing to contradict the murder-suicide theory. Meanwhile, relative Ann Eaton cannot believe Sheila was responsible, and discusses her growing suspicions with her brother David Boutflour(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: A report on the downward trend in spending in Britain's high-street stores over the past 10 years. Geraint Vincent follows typical shopping street Dalton Road in Barrow-in-Furness through the crucial festive period and hears from firms facing high rates and tough competition from the internet and retail parks. But to some, high streets are more than just a place of business - they are a crucial part of the community. Geraint also meets those trying to attract more visitors, and explores whether everyone has a responsibility to support town centres(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: For the final leg of his journey, Gino D'Acampo takes a railway adventure to picturesque Lake Iseo, a hidden gem often overshadowed by its big sisters Garda and Como. He starts out angling for sardines, then takes his catch and prepares a dish of grilled fish for the passengers of Treno dei Sapore - a tourist train that takes guests along the lakeside and into the Franciacorta region. While they enjoy the views they also get a seven-course meal - with today's starter cooked by Gino(n)

programme.title: Welcome to HMP Belmarsh with Ross Kemp
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ross Kemp's report from inside the maximum-security jail takes him to Belmarsh's jail-within-a-jail, where anti-Islamic inmates have to be separated from Islamic prisoners for fear of violence. He also heads to the prison healthcare unit, and meets transgender prisoner Claire Darbyshire, who was given one of the lowest minimum terms for murder in British legal history after killing her father(n)

programme.title: The Late Debate
programme.desc: Simon Harris is joined by a panel of local MPs for a discussion on the latest news and events at Westminster(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a student from a prestigious all-female college is found dead, Will and Geordie infiltrate the murky world of campus politics and university societies to find the killer. Navigating strange initiations, raucous parties - and with investigative journalist, Ellie Harding, hot on their heels - they need to use every advantage at their disposal to unravel the mystery. Cathy's ambitions for a life of her own start to test the delicate family balance, while fresh from a spell with Daniel in Marrakech, Leonard finds it hard to acclimatise to life back in Grantchester. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia visits the lush Ionian island of Corfu, beginning in its capital, Corfu Town, where she discovers a surprising, cosmopolitan city more like a little slice of Italy than Greece. After sampling the delights of its famous mumquat flavoured ice-cream, a cookery lesson in making signature savoury dish pastitsada reveals the island's centuries-old love affair with exotic spices. Julia meets a pair of brothers passionate about revolutionising production of Corfu's oldest and most important export - olive oil- and ends her stay trying out the secret to staying young that all Corfiots swear by(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: After Will and Leonard witness a deadly and deliberate hit-and-run, Geordie traces the car to a pair of decidedly dysfunctional brothers, whose house lies far off the beaten path. The victim's wife, his employee and the two brothers are all damaged characters caught in a web of lies and betrayals - and it's not just secrets hiding within the siblings' grounds. Back at home, Will and Geordie both struggle as unwelcome guests look set to stay - Will's mum Amelia seems to have found a carbon copy of his late father in her new beau. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen with guests Angela Thorne, Ronald Pickup and Samuel West(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Chief Superintendent Jack Cornish goes missing and a recently embalmed body is discovered near an isolated farm track - could there be a link? Guest starring Lucy Speed and Peter Davison, with Kevin Whately and Babou Ceesay(n)

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: With Nick seriously injured in hospital, Lisa furiously seeks out Sean, while Med feels increasingly side-lined. Sean's revelations lead the team to suspect Nick might have vital information about Holly and Dylan and they desperately wait for him to regain consciousness. Meanwhile, Rob attempts to leave the online forum, but finds that it is not as easy as he had hoped, and Sam tries to offer Abbie a way out from Vincent. Starring Morven Christie, Jonas Armstrong and Matthew McNulty(n)

programme.desc: Walker investigates when sibling rivalry and greed result in a fatal car crash. A devout couple are faced with money problems when their baby is diagnosed with a life-threatening condition. Meanwhile, Peggy is up to her usual tricks as she frightens some holiday-makers, and Jack and Gina get a scare of their own during a walk on the moors. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor, Murray Head and Tricia Penrose(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tensions rise in the courtroom on the first day of the hearing for Alice. Joe and Rachel discuss the future, and there might be romance on the cards for Lynn(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Alice's future is in the balance as Nick takes to the stand in court, while Vic discovers that Reg has left a dilemma for the family. Luke and Biff fish for evidence against Eric(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre is thrilled to receive a passionate letter from Samir, while Liz advises her not to let a holiday romance ruin her life. Alf shows Maureen the ropes at the Cash and Carry(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre wants to see Samir, but has no money to send him for a ticket, so she asks Mike for a loan. Des is amazed when Sean offers him a job(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Larry moves back into the family's villa having come down with mumps, which Louisa hopes will give Aunt Hermione an incentive to cancel her planned visit. Her hopes prove in vain when her overbearing relative turns up, accompanied by medium Mrs Haddock (Una Stubbs), who claims to be able to contact Louisa's husband. She tells Louisa that he is terribly unhappy about her numerous male admirers, fuelling her doubts over her relationship with Hugh, and proposes a seance on a stormy night to set his troubled spirit at rest(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker investigates when sibling rivalry and greed result in a fatal car crash. A devout couple are faced with money problems when their baby is diagnosed with a life-threatening condition. Meanwhile, Peggy is up to her usual tricks as she frightens some holiday-makers, and Jack and Gina get a scare of their own during a walk on the moors. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor, Murray Head and Tricia Penrose(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle responds to complaints of sabotage at an RAF burns hospital and uncovers adultery and murder within the medical unit. As the hunt for the guilty party intensifies, the detective discovers his son Andrew could be a suspect and is shocked when he hears he has been reported missing from his barracks. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, John Wood, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The irascible medic decides to move out of the surgery to give Louisa space, leaving Mrs Tishell concerned he will not be able to look after himself on his own. Elsewhere, PC Penhale helps Janice babysit, and Al is very concerned when he finds out about Bert's behaviour and hears there are bailiffs at the restaurant(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin have their first therapy session together - though neither of them was expecting they would be given homework - while Morwenna is not happy about it when a teenager on work experience starts trying to boss her around in the surgery. Mrs Tishell is surprised when her husband Clive returns and tells her he wants to give things another go, and Louisa has to call Martin when one of her pupils collapses during a school excursion. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: A confession to Lisa shines a possible new light on Dylan's killer. Meanwhile, Abbie has a terrifying experience with some local addicts, and Rob's escalating actions begin to impact on his family. Elsewhere, Med starts to suspect Lisa is hiding something from him and begins to make his own investigations. Six-part crime drama following the work of Detective Sergeant Lisa Armstrong as she is assigned to a missing persons investigation. Starring Morven Christie, Jonas Armstrong, Tracie Bennett and Lindsey Coulson(n)

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: Manning and Med question Holly about the mysterious other person present when Dylan died, determined to get to the truth. Lisa struggles to accept her suspension from the force after the reveal of her secret, but with everything at stake, will she still be able to bring Dylan's killer to justice from the sidelines? Final installment of the crime drama, starring Morven Christie and Jonas Armstrong(n)
